steps.title: Use the vertical shift
steps.content: The graph of $y=f(x)+5$ is the graph of $y=f(x)$ shifted up $5$ units. Every point keeps the same $x$-coordinate, and its $y$-coordinate increases by $5$. The shape and domain stay the same.

steps.title: Check the asymptote
steps.content:
The original graph approaches $y=0$. After shifting up $5$ units, the transformed graph approaches
$$y=0+5=5$$
So the matching graph must have horizontal asymptote $y=5$.

steps.title: Verify a listed point
steps.content:
The original graph includes the point $(3,3)$. After shifting up $5$ units, that point becomes
$$(3,3+5)=(3,8)$$
Choices with asymptote $y=0$ or $y=-5$ cannot match this vertical shift of the whole graph. This listed point and the horizontal asymptote match the same graph. The answer is D.
